citation_audit.farsi_text: روایت زید بن ارقم عن النبی صلی اللّه علیه و سلم (انی تارك فیکم الثقلین ما ان تمسکتم بهما لن تضلوا بعدی احدهما اعظم من الآخر کتاب اللّه و عترتی)
citation_audit.english: Narration from Zaid bin Arqam from the Prophet (peace be upon him): 'I am leaving among you the two weighty things; as long as you hold fast to them, you will not go astray after me. One of them is greater than the other: the Book of Allah and my progeny.'
citation_audit.notes: Bare hadith text in Arabic with companion named, but no source book, chain, or evidence for 'sahih' claim. References 'احادیث سابقه' vaguely.

citation_audit.farsi_text: (علیکم بسنتی وسنة الخلفاء الراشدین المهدیین من بعدی تمسکوا بها و عضوا علیها تالتواعید)
citation_audit.english: (Hold fast to my Sunnah and the Sunnah of the Rightly Guided Caliphs after me; cling to it and beware of innovations.)
citation_audit.notes: Bare hadith text only; no source, chain, or authentication.

citation_audit.english: (Take half of your religion from this red-haired woman [pointing to Aisha].)
citation_audit.notes: Bare text; 'sahih' claimed without evidence or source.
Bare text; no source or authentication.

citation_audit.english: (Hold fast to the covenant of Ibn Am Abd [Abu Bakr].)

citation_audit.farsi_text: (رضیت لکم ما رضی لکم ابن ام عبد)
citation_audit.english: (I am pleased for you what Ibn Am Abd [Abu Bakr] was pleased for you.)

citation_audit.farsi_text: (اعلمکم بالحلال و الحرام معاذ بن جبل)
citation_audit.english: (Muadh bin Jabal is the most knowledgeable of you regarding halal and haram.)
(Follow those after me: Abu Bakr and Umar.)
citation_audit.authentication_evidence: بدرجه شهرت و تواتر معنوی رسیده
citation_audit.notes: Bare text; mutawatir (moral) asserted without proof or sources.

citation_audit.english: (The consultation is only for the Muhajirun and Ansar.)
citation_audit.notes: Bare text from Ali; 'sahih and mutawatir among Shia' claimed without evidence.

citation_audit.farsi_text: (مثل اهل بیتی فیکم مثل سفينة نوح من رکبها نجی ومن تخلف عنها غرق)
citation_audit.english: (The example of my Ahl al-Bayt among you is like Noah's ark: whoever rides it is saved, and whoever stays behind drowns.)
citation_audit.notes: Bare text; referred to as 'حدیث' but no source.

citation_audit.farsi_text: (.. تون فص الکتاب وکین یَغْض ..* الاية. البقرة: ۸۵)
citation_audit.english: (... part of the Book and then disbelieve in ... [Al-Baqarah 85])
citation_audit.notes: Quranic citation verifiable (likely 2:85 on partial belief).

citation_audit.farsi_text: لا نرق ین آحٍَ ین رس ...۷ الاّبة. البقرة: ۲۸۵
citation_audit.english: (We make no distinction between any of His messengers ... [Al-Baqarah 285])

citation_audit.farsi_text: (... یِکل جَعَلنا نکم شَرعة ومنهاجا ...۸ الاية. المائدة: 4۸)
citation_audit.english: (For each [community] We appointed a code and a way ... [Al-Ma'idah 48])

citation_audit.farsi_text: لازم آید که جمیع اقارب آنحضرت صلی له علیه و سلم ائمه باشند واجب الاطاعت علی الخصوص مثل عبد الله بن عباس و محمد بن الحنفیه و زید بن علی و حسن مشنی و اسحاق این حعفر صادق و امثال ایشان
citation_audit.english: It becomes necessary that all relatives of the Prophet (PBUH) be imams and specifically obligatory to obey, like Abdullah bin Abbas, Muhammad bin al-Hanafiyyah, Zaid bin Ali, etc.
citation_audit.notes: Premises: Thaqalayn implies obedience to all progeny; lists non-Imami figures. Logic valid reductio but assumes universal application without hidden qualifiers. No fallacies.

citation_audit.farsi_text: تشبیه اهل بیت درین حدیث بسفینه اقتضا می نماید که محبت جمیم اهل بیت و انباع کل ایشان در نجات و فلاح ضرور نیست ... شیعه چون متمسک ببعض اهل سنت شدند ... ناجی باشند
citation_audit.english: Analogy of Ahl al-Bayt to ship does not require love/following all for salvation; Shia following some are saved. Sunni rebuttal in text.
citation_audit.notes: Premises: Partial ship occupancy suffices; rebuttal exposes fallacy of composition (partial ≠ whole) and leads to contradictions in Shia sects. Valid logic; no major fallacies.

citation_audit.farsi_text: بخلاف شیعه که هیچ فرقه ایشان جمیع اهل بیت را دوست ندار و بعضی یک طایفه را محبوب می سازند و بقیه را مبغوض میدارند
citation_audit.english: Unlike Shia, no sect loves all Ahl al-Bayt equally; some love one group, hate others.
citation_audit.authentication_evidence: چنانچه کتب تفسیر و حدیث و فقه ایشان بران گواه است
citation_audit.notes: Broad claim about Shia books; no specific citations provided.

overall_evidentiary_assessment.assessment: Poor evidentiary rigor: All hadiths are bare quotes without source books, full chains, or volume/page references. Authenticity claims (sahih, mutawatir) are asserted without proof. Quran citations are properly referenced. Logical arguments are coherent but polemical, relying on unsourced hadiths. No grade A sourcing; typical of polemical texts lacking academic standards.
